title: pelargonium × subuniflorum
intro: Pelargonium × subuniflorum, also known as Cape Geranium or Notho Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the Pelargonium family, specifically a cross between Pelargonium capitatum and Pelargonium cucullatum. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of volatile oils. The health benefits of Pelargonium × subuniflorum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as asthma and bronchitis. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as tea, tincture, and essential oil, which can be consumed or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
